_comment: Source of truth for briefing-slides skill decks. Hand-editable. Regenerate decks with `node scripts/generate-briefing-decks.mjs`. Re-extract from legacy decks with `node scripts/extract-deck-manifest.mjs` (overwrites this file).
generated_from: briefing-slides/afps-skill-*.html (legacy template) + index.html grouping + SKILL.md frontmatter

decks.title: customer-discovery
decks.subtitle: Business AFPS / Concept and market
decks.blurb: Define ICP and customer evidence before market decisions.
decks.lead: Orchestrator — detect pre-product vs product-exists mode, bootstrap ICP candidates, recommend customer-discovery frameworks, synthesize outputs into unified ICP research

whenToUse.runWhen: Use after a concept exists and before market, journey, or UX decisions harden.
whenToUse.doNotExpect: This is not a shortcut around its upstream evidence. If required artifacts are missing, the skill should route to the prerequisite instead.
whenToUse.primaryInput: Current repo artifacts plus the previous AFPS phase's research, design, prototype, spec, or task output.
whenToUse.primaryQuestion: What decision, artifact, or proof does this step need to make safe before the next skill?

expect.output: ICP candidates and customer evidence synthesized into research.
expect.detailLives: Dense artifacts stay in research, design, prototype, specs, tasks, or source files. This deck is only the overview layer.
expect.qualityBar: The output should make the next route more obvious and reduce ambiguity, not create a new parallel workflow.
expect.humanReview: Expect either a checkpoint, alignment page, evidence artifact, prototype inspection, or shipping summary depending on the skill.

handoff.before: Use after a concept exists and before market, journey, or UX decisions harden.
handoff.seriesPoint: Explain why this skill exists as a boundary: what risk it burns down before the next AFPS step.

gate.question: Approve whether this deck accurately explains what people can expect from customer-discovery.

decks.title: ux-variations
decks.subtitle: Business AFPS / Product shape
decks.blurb: Explore alternate progression branches for one named user flow.
decks.lead: Interview and plan multiple UX and UI variations for a product, page, or flow, including onboarding, typical workflows, sharing, collaboration, return use, and interface alternatives users can compare before locking a direction — and concrete visual/layout UI variations (component choices, spatial arrangements, information density)

whenToUse.runWhen: Use when a mapped flow needs alternatives before visual decisions lock.
whenToUse.doNotExpect: This is not a shortcut around its upstream evidence. If required artifacts are missing, the skill should route to the prerequisite instead.

whenToUse.primaryQuestion: What decision, artifact, or proof does this step need to make safe before the next skill?

expect.output: Multiple progression branches for a named user flow before one becomes a UI direction.
expect.detailLives: Dense artifacts stay in research, design, prototype, specs, tasks, or source files. This deck is only the overview layer.
expect.qualityBar: The output should make the next route more obvious and reduce ambiguity, not create a new parallel workflow.
expect.humanReview: Expect either a checkpoint, alignment page, evidence artifact, prototype inspection, or shipping summary depending on the skill.

decks.title: ui-interview
decks.subtitle: Business AFPS / Product shape
decks.blurb: Specify visual UI details for a selected UX variation.
decks.lead: Interview human-visible UI page by page to define a complete UI specification, including layout, hierarchy, controls, links, spacing, sizing, responsive behavior, visual states, diagnostics/audit screens, and implementation-ready interface details — supports a requirements-only mode that establishes data, actions, and states without locking layout or component decisions

whenToUse.runWhen: Use after a UX variation branch is ready for visual treatment.
whenToUse.doNotExpect: This is not a shortcut around its upstream evidence. If required artifacts are missing, the skill should route to the prerequisite instead.
whenToUse.primaryInput: Current repo artifacts plus the previous AFPS phase's research, design, prototype, spec, or task output.
whenToUse.primaryQuestion: What decision, artifact, or proof does this step need to make safe before the next skill?

expect.output: A page-by-page UI spec with layout, controls, responsive behavior, and visual states.
expect.detailLives: Dense artifacts stay in research, design, prototype, specs, tasks, or source files. This deck is only the overview layer.
expect.qualityBar: The output should make the next route more obvious and reduce ambiguity, not create a new parallel workflow.
expect.humanReview: Expect either a checkpoint, alignment page, evidence artifact, prototype inspection, or shipping summary depending on the skill.

handoff.before: Use after a UX variation branch is ready for visual treatment.
handoff.seriesPoint: Explain why this skill exists as a boundary: what risk it burns down before the next AFPS step.

decks.title: build-ui-screens
decks.subtitle: Business AFPS / Prototype proof
decks.blurb: Build visual screens for an approved UI branch.
decks.lead: Build the visual UI screens for one approved UI branch as an ordered element-batch loop — one flow step at a time, with a per-batch visual checkpoint and a minimum-UI stop — using fake, fixture, local, or in-memory data, then hand the screens to logic-wiring to make them clickable.

whenToUse.runWhen: Use after a UI branch is approved and needs concrete screens before wiring.
whenToUse.doNotExpect: This is not a shortcut around its upstream evidence. If required artifacts are missing, the skill should route to the prerequisite instead.

whenToUse.primaryQuestion: What decision, artifact, or proof does this step need to make safe before the next skill?

expect.output: Visual screens for one approved UI branch, built with local or fixture data.
expect.detailLives: Dense artifacts stay in research, design, prototype, specs, tasks, or source files. This deck is only the overview layer.
expect.qualityBar: The output should make the next route more obvious and reduce ambiguity, not create a new parallel workflow.
expect.humanReview: Expect either a checkpoint, alignment page, evidence artifact, prototype inspection, or shipping summary depending on the skill.

handoff.before: Use after a UI branch is approved and needs concrete screens before wiring.
handoff.seriesPoint: Explain why this skill exists as a boundary: what risk it burns down before the next AFPS step.

decks.title: logic-wiring
decks.subtitle: Business AFPS / Prototype proof
decks.blurb: Wire screens and channel behavior into a clickable prototype.
